description: Contribution-point manifest for the SDLC. Each phase is a plugin-internal contribution point: a skill (the worker instructions), a command (the manual entry point), a named roster (the agents fanned out), an activation predicate (when the orchestrator may enter here), and an exit gate (the artifact that must exist before advancing). Add a phase by adding a skill + agent(s) + one entry here. Core files never change. This is the same lazy-activation / contribution-point pattern the plugin teaches for target systems.

phases.title: Research & Discovery
phases.activation: intent matches investigate|research|discover|evaluate|compare|unknown-domain OR upstream unknowns block planning
phases.exit_criteria: One citation-anchored brief per open question; each cited claim has >=2 modalities OR is explicitly marked low-confidence; key unknowns resolved or deferred with rationale.
phases.skippable_when: goal is fully specified and domain is well understood (record the skip rationale in the run log)

phases.title: Planning
phases.activation: research artifacts exist OR goal is well specified
phases.exit_criteria: A plan with a task DAG (nodes = tasks, edges = dependencies), success criteria, and a mapping of each task to a target interface for later evidence.

phases.title: Architecting
phases.activation: a plan exists AND the work introduces or changes structure/interfaces
phases.exit_criteria: One ADR per non-trivial decision (context, options, decision, consequences). If target is an extensible/plugin system, the pluggable-architecture principles checklist is applied and recorded.
phases.skippable_when: change is small and introduces no new interfaces or cross-cutting structure

phases.title: Implementation
phases.activation: a plan (and ADRs when applicable) exist
phases.exit_criteria: All plan tasks implemented; per-task implementation notes record files touched and blast radius; code compiles/builds where a build exists.

phases.title: Review
phases.exit_criteria: spec-reviewer, code-quality-reviewer, and security-reviewer have each reported; every finding is resolved or explicitly waived with rationale; no unresolved blocker-severity findings.

phases.title: Testing
phases.exit_criteria: A test suite exists and runs; each expected behavior from the plan maps to at least one test; suite is green or every failure is triaged.

phases.title: Evidence-Based Hard Quality Gate
phases.activation: always required before a run may complete
phases.exit_criteria: The software was exercised AS A USER over the detected interface; every expected behavior has expected-vs-actual + at least one artifact; evidence status is pass; the file is fresh (newer than the latest source change in this run).

gates.artifact-exists: The phase's artifact_glob matches at least one file created during this run.
gates.artifact-exists-and-no-open-blockers: artifact-exists AND no review finding with severity=blocker remains status=open.
gates.evidence-pass: The latest .sdlc/evidence/<run>.json has status=pass, as_user=true, every behavior pass=true with >=1 artifact, and generated_at is newer than the newest source mtime for this run. Enforced by hooks/quality-gate; not advisory.
